code.description: Stable machine-readable subcode (lower_snake_case). SDKs should switch on this, not `message`.

message.description: Human-readable summary. Not stable; do not parse.

request_id.description: Echo of the `X-Request-Id` response header for support.

ApiErrorEnvelope.description: Wire format for every non-2xx public API response.
ApiErrorEnvelope.title: ApiErrorEnvelope

Avatar.description: Public Avatar resource returned by `GET /v1/avatars` and `GET /v1/avatars/{id}`.

default_custom_instructions.description: Preferred managed-conversation instructions for this avatar, when configured.

default_voice.description: Preferred conversation voice for this avatar, when configured.

failure_reason.description: Human-readable reason the build failed (e.g. `No face detected in source image.`). Only set while `status` is `failed`.

id.description: Stable `av_…` avatar id. Platform stock avatars use fixed ids such as `av_stock_001`; customer avatars use generated ids.

Avatar.title: Avatar

next_cursor.description: Pass as `starting_after` on the next request to continue.

AvatarStatus.description:
Lifecycle of a custom avatar.

`ready` — usable for sessions.
`processing` — being fitted / converted.
`failed` — fitting failed; will not be usable without re-upload.
AvatarStatus.title: AvatarStatus

ErrorType.description:
Top-level error categories. Maps roughly to HTTP status.

`code` (a free-form lower_snake_case string on `ApiError`) is the
machine-readable subcode SDKs should switch on; `type` is the broad
category.
ErrorType.title: ErrorType

LiveKitAudioSource.description:
How the LiveKit worker receives audio from the room.

Use `data_stream` with the LiveKit Agents plugin. Use `track` only for
custom integrations that publish audio tracks directly.
LiveKitAudioSource.title: LiveKitAudioSource

LiveKitTransportConfig.description:
BYO LiveKit transport.

The customer owns the room and mints `worker_token`; we never touch
their LiveKit API key or secret.

audio_source.description: How the worker receives audio. See LiveKitAudioSource.

room_name.description: Name of the LiveKit room the worker should join.

subscribe_to_identity.description: When `audio_source=track`, pin to this participant. Ignored otherwise.

synthetic_audio_if_no_input.description: If True and no participant joins within 10 s, the worker falls back to a synthetic sine generator. Demo aid only; default off.

worker_identity.description: Identity the worker presents inside the room.

worker_token.description: Short-lived JWT the customer mints authorizing our worker to join the room. **Write-only.** Never echoed back on read; `GET /v1/sessions/{id}.transport.worker_token` returns the string `"[redacted]"`.

LiveKitTransportConfig.title: LiveKitTransportConfig

PipecatSessionCreateRequest.description: Start request for a Pipecat avatar media session.
PipecatSessionCreateRequest.title: PipecatSessionCreateRequest

PipecatTransportConfig.description:
Reserved — BYO Pipecat transport.

`POST /v1/sessions` still rejects `transport.type=pipecat`; use
`POST /v1/pipecat/sessions` for the Protoface Pipecat video service.
PipecatTransportConfig.title: PipecatTransportConfig

PipecatWebSocketRelayView.description: Short-lived direct media connection details for the Pipecat plugin.
PipecatWebSocketRelayView.title: PipecatWebSocketRelayView

avatar_id.description: Stable `av_…` avatar id. Must reference a platform stock avatar or customer avatar the caller's API key can access.

idle_timeout_seconds.description: End the session automatically after this many seconds without received audio. Only applies to transports that observe inbound audio; ignored otherwise.

max_duration_seconds.description: Optional customer-requested hard ceiling on session duration. The API clamps this to the caller's plan limit and returns the effective value on the Session resource.

metadata.description: Customer-owned free-form JSON, <= 8 KB. Keys prefixed with `_avatar.` are managed by Protoface.

quality.description: Quality tier; drives the billing multiplier.

transport.description: Discriminated union of transport configs, keyed by `type`.

next_cursor.description: Pass as `starting_after` on the next request to continue.

SessionStatus.description:
Public session lifecycle.

Terminal states are `ended`, `failed`, `canceled`. `ending` is the
graceful-drain state while a worker finishes publishing in-flight frames.
SessionStatus.title: SessionStatus

SessionUsage.description:
Live usage counters embedded in the Session resource.

Eventually-consistent — lags the latest worker heartbeat by up to
one interval. Canonical billing data lives in `UsageEvent`.
SessionUsage.title: SessionUsage

billable_seconds.description: Total billable seconds across all sessions in the window.

by_quality.description: `billable_seconds` per quality tier. Forward-compatible breakdown — additional `by_*` slices may appear over time.

period_end.description: Exclusive end of the aggregated window. Matches the `period_end` query argument.

period_start.description: Inclusive start of the aggregated window. Matches the `period_start` query argument.

sessions.description: Number of sessions that contributed usage in the window.

WebSocketTransportConfig.description:
Reserved — raw WebSocket transport. **Not yet available.**

Listed in the schema so existing clients keep parsing future responses,
but `POST /v1/sessions` rejects it today with
`invalid_request` / `code=transport.unsupported`.
WebSocketTransportConfig.title: WebSocketTransportConfig

ApiKey.description: Live API key minted in the dashboard. Pass as `Authorization: Bearer sk_live_…`. Keys are scoped to a single environment (staging / prod).

info.description:
Create realtime avatar sessions.

### Authentication
All `/v1/*` endpoints require a live API key:

```
Authorization: Bearer sk_live_...
```

Keys are minted in the dashboard and are scoped to a single environment
(staging or prod).

### Idempotency
`POST /v1/sessions` may include an `Idempotency-Key` header (any opaque string,
<= 255 chars). Retries with the same key inside 24 hours return the first
response. The header is optional.

### Errors
Every non-2xx response is a JSON body of the form:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "invalid_request",
    "code": "transport.unsupported",
    "message": "transport.type=pipecat is not supported",
    "param": "transport.type",
    "request_id": "req_01HXY..."
  }
}
```

Switch on `error.code` (stable lower_snake_case), not `error.message`.

### Pagination
List endpoints use cursor pagination:

```
GET /v1/sessions?limit=20&starting_after=sess_01HXY...
```

Response shape:

```json
{
  "object": "list",
  "data": [...],
  "has_more": true,
  "next_cursor": "sess_01HXY..."
}
```
info.summary: Protoface — realtime avatar API for developers.
info.title: Protoface API

get.description: List avatars available to the calling org. Includes platform-built-in stock avatars (e.g. `av_stock_001`) and any customer-uploaded avatars.

parameters.description: Pagination cursor — an `av_…` id from a previous page.
parameters.name: starting_after

schema.description: Pagination cursor — an `av_…` id from a previous page.
schema.title: Starting After

parameters.description: Which avatars to return: `all` (default — platform stock plus your org's), `platform` (only built-in stock), or `org` (only your custom).
parameters.name: scope

schema.description: Which avatars to return: `all` (default — platform stock plus your org's), `platform` (only built-in stock), or `org` (only your custom).
schema.title: Scope

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on avatar name or id.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on avatar name or id.
schema.title: Q

422.description: Request body or parameters failed validation.

post.description: Upload a portrait image to build a custom avatar. Returns the avatar in `processing`; poll `GET /v1/avatars/{id}` until it is `ready` (or `failed`). `multipart/form-data`: `image` (PNG/JPEG), `name`, and optional `runtime_type` (default `avtr1`).
post.summary: Create a custom avatar from an image

delete.description: Delete a custom avatar and permanently remove its uploaded source image and built assets. The avatar disappears from listings and can no longer run sessions; past session and usage records are kept. Platform stock avatars cannot be deleted.

422.description: Request body or parameters failed validation.

get.description: Return a single avatar by id.

422.description: Request body or parameters failed validation.

get.description: Return the current Protoface billing plan catalog. This endpoint is unauthenticated so signup and pricing UI can render the same catalog used by checkout and enforcement.

422.description: Request body or parameters failed validation.

post.description: Start a Protoface avatar session for Pipecat. The response contains short-lived WebSocket media credentials used by the `pipecat-protoface` package.

422.description: Request body or parameters failed validation.

post.summary: Create a Pipecat avatar media session

get.description: List sessions for the calling org, newest first. Repeatable `status` filters OR together; the group aliases `active` and `terminal` expand to the underlying status set. `created_after` / `created_before` accept ISO-8601 UTC. Paginate with `limit` + `starting_after=<last_session_id>`.

parameters.description: Repeatable filter. Accepts a `SessionStatus` value or the aliases `active` / `terminal`. Multiple values OR together.
parameters.name: status

schema.description: Repeatable filter. Accepts a `SessionStatus` value or the aliases `active` / `terminal`. Multiple values OR together.
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter to sessions referencing this avatar.
parameters.name: avatar_id

schema.description: Filter to sessions referencing this avatar.
schema.title: Avatar Id

parameters.description: Pagination cursor — a `sess_…` id from a previous page.
parameters.name: starting_after

schema.description: Pagination cursor — a `sess_…` id from a previous page.
schema.title: Starting After

422.description: Request body or parameters failed validation.

post.description:
Allocate a worker and begin a realtime avatar session.

Returns immediately with `status: queued`; poll `GET /v1/sessions/{id}` (or wait for the `session.first_frame` webhook) to see the worker pick the job up. Sessions count against your plan's concurrency cap; a 503 response with `code=at_capacity` and a `Retry-After` header means there are no free workers right now.

get.description: Return the current state of a session, including timing and usage counters.

422.description: Request body or parameters failed validation.

post.description: Terminate a session immediately. Idempotent: calling on an already-terminal session returns 200 with the existing state. The session transitions to `canceled` if it had not yet started, or `ending` (then `ended` once the worker drains) if it was running.

422.description: Request body or parameters failed validation.

get.description: Public status of the Protoface platform. Polled by the status page and operator dashboards. Unauthenticated.

Return aggregated usage for the calling org over a time range.

Pass either `period=current_month` (or `previous_month`) for convenience, **or** an explicit `period_start` / `period_end` pair. The two modes are mutually exclusive.

parameters.description: Convenience period selector; mutually exclusive with explicit range.
parameters.name: period

schema.description: Convenience period selector; mutually exclusive with explicit range.
schema.title: Period

parameters.description: ISO-8601 UTC. Must be paired with `period_end`.
parameters.name: period_start

schema.description: ISO-8601 UTC. Must be paired with `period_end`.
schema.title: Period Start

parameters.description: ISO-8601 UTC. Must be paired with `period_start`.
parameters.name: period_end

schema.description: ISO-8601 UTC. Must be paired with `period_start`.
schema.title: Period End

422.description: Request body or parameters failed validation.

tags.description: Create, retrieve, list, and end realtime avatar sessions. Sessions stream audio in and video out; their lifecycle drives billing.
tags.name: sessions

tags.description: Avatar definitions. Includes platform stock avatars and customer-uploaded avatars.
tags.name: avatars

tags.description: Public platform metadata — currently just status. Unauthenticated. Internal load-balancer and task health probes exist on this host but are not part of the documented surface.
tags.name: platform

tags.description: Direct media sessions for the `pipecat-protoface` avatar video service.
tags.name: pipecat

tags.description: Public billing plan metadata for signup and pricing UI.
tags.name: billing
